narrative:
Kevin De Bruyne is a central attacking midfielder for Belgium, consistently performing at an elite level. His overall rating of 7.44/10 ranks him in the top 1% of attacking midfields at this tournament. This high rating is a blend of his exceptional club form over the last two seasons and his current World Cup performances.

De Bruyne's attacking prowess is evident with 3.673 shots per 90 minutes and 3.306 key passes per 90 minutes at this World Cup. His expected assists (xA) per 90 minutes at club level is 0.405, underscoring his creative vision. A notable area for less contribution is his defensive work, with 0.735 tackles per 90 minutes during the tournament.

De Bruyne serves as the primary offensive architect for his national team, with 48% of his rating directly from this World Cup. Scouts and fans should continue to watch for his high volume of key passes and shots, which are crucial to Belgium's attacking strategy. His influence on the final third will be pivotal in their remaining matches.
